name: Light on Two Sides of Every Room
paraphrase: Locate each room so that it has outdoor space outside it on at least two sides, and place windows in those outdoor walls so that natural light enters from more than one direction. Alexander observes that when people are free to choose, they consistently gravitate toward rooms lit from two sides and abandon rooms lit from only one; rooms with cross-light have less glare, faces and gestures read more clearly, and the social atmosphere is markedly better.
